file.content:
new `config` command (print, `--home`, `--validate`), init's
  commented `.vc-config.toml` defaults, and bot-session's
  `--result-lines`/`--col-width` config layer all derive from it,
  so they can't drift. Also a `notes/transcript-format.md` SSOT +
  sample for the bot-session format, and a sweep retiring the
  ambiguous "dotted" wording [[7]]
- bot-session: --fields / --unknown output clarification — the
  inventory views are now documented rather than opaque:
  [transcript-format.md](notes/transcript-format.md) defines
  entry / entry type and the `.`/`[]` field notation with a
  bot-session example (0.71.0-8), and the ambiguous "dotted"
  wording was retired (0.71.0-7). In-view column labeling
  (headers / a legend) left as an optional nicety
- docs: shared protocol sync + jj refactor plan — adopted the vc-template-x1 shared notes set (AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, versioning.md, jj-tips.md) with vc-x1's 0.69.0 corrections ratified template-side (manifest: [notes-sync-20260716.md](notes/notes-sync-20260716.md)); jj facade → jj-lib refactor program planned in [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md), absorbing eight Todos [[1]]
- docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md — todo list moved from notes/ to the conventional root-file family; live references swept (AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, README, ARCHITECTURE, notes/*); no-arg validate-todo / fix-todo default follows the move; historical files keep `notes/todo.md`; the shared doc set diverges until vc-template-x1 and iiac-perf apply the same change [[2]]
- feat: bot-session --col-width knob — the field views'
  (`--fields`/`--unknown`/`--per-line`) first-column pad
  becomes `--col-width N`, default widened 44 → 68 (aligns
  the type column for ~99% of observed key paths; only the
  long-tail `snapshot.trackedFileBackups.<abs path>.*` keys
  overflow); config-hierarchy resolution deferred to Todo #12 [[6]]
- feat: bot-session --fields + --raw explorer — bot-session
  doubles as a schema explorer: --fields (dotted-path
  inventory per entry type: count, kinds, samples),
  --unknown (inventory minus the extractor's KNOWN_PATHS —
  the unmodeled surface; 132 paths on first real run),
  --raw (pretty-printed source lines); --per-line (a fields
  section per source line, composes with --unknown); --lines
  unified to source-JSONL-line units in every view,
  conversation included; drift-over-time baseline deferred to the
  discovery/index cycle [[5]]
- feat: bot-session --result-lines knob — the [result]-body
  cap becomes a flag: `--result-lines N` (default 10, 0 =
  unlimited), Output-range help group; was hardwired to 10
  even under --all [[4]]
- feat: bot-session transcript viewer — display a session transcript as a conversation: two-layer tolerant parse (serde_json text → Value; hand extraction into our structs, raw retained), eight-item composable output (--<item> / --no-<item> / --all / --none) with git-style config defaults (CLI > .vc-config.toml > user config > built-in), --lines slicing, UTC headers; --raw and index view deferred (Todo #12), EPIPE logger panic recorded (Bugs #4) [[3]]

`tests/common/mod.rs` instead, with the why documented
  - the shared helpers spawn directly rather than calling
    `crate::jj`, so test inspection stays independent of
    the facade under test
- [[6]] 0.73.0 refactor: DRY jj facade (close-out)
  - ARCHITECTURE.md: `src/jj.rs` joins the module
    inventory; the stale subcommand enumeration corrected
    (`finalize` dropped; `squash_push`, `revert`,
    `validate_bot`, `config_cmd`, `bot_session` /
    `transcript`, `sync/state` added)
  - per-stage refactor Todos: each remaining program stage
    is a short ranked entry pointing at its stage section
    (por → dual to todo-backlog); the umbrella entry
    shrinks to the program pointer
  - `## Done` entry; `## In Progress` retired; ladder
    commit refs pruned (the As-built ladder here is the
    permanent record)

### Outcome

Every read-only jj query spawn now goes through `src/jj.rs`
(log templates + bookmark listings), with one tracking
parser pair and one ochid trailer parser beside it, and one
jj test helper per crate. The remaining `run(` sites are
mutations (commit / describe / bookmark / push / fetch /
squash / rebase / op), the git/gh spawns owned by the
de-gitify-init stage and the by-design `gh` calls, push's
`jj diff --stat` display passthrough, and the deliberate
direct spawns in test harnesses — the surface the jj-lib
migration stage inherits.

[1]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/f761e89092df "f761e89092dfbb82e8ab355d6e5a058e77b07e23"

# Chores-14

Continuation of `chores-13.md` (closed at `0.72.x`, parked on
the `support-trapezoid-commits` bookmark). This file covers
the jj refactor program
([refactor-20260716.md](../refactor-20260716.md)) from
`0.73.0` onward, worked on the `refactor-vc-x1` bookmark
while `main` parks at the `0.71.0` tip.

Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`AGENTS.md`](../../AGENTS.md#reference-numbering); chores-14
starts at `[1]`.

## refactor: DRY jj facade

Commits: see [As-built ladder](#as-built-ladder)

~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,
"--no-graph", "-T", <template>, "-R", <repo>])` and each
module has quietly grown a private wrapper — spotted at
0.69.0-2, where `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind"
but was the Nth reinvention. One typed facade module ends
the reinvention; first stage of the refactor program.

### As-built ladder

- [[1]] 0.73.0-0 chore: open jj facade cycle
  - restore the five notes files from
    `support-trapezoid-commits` (TODO.md, todo-backlog,
    forks-multi-user, refactor doc, chores-13) so the shared
    bookkeeping rides the refactor line
  - re-plan after the pivot: `## In Progress` → the DRY
    facade ladder; the trapezoid `--merge [<base>]` design
    folded into the refactor doc's trapezoid stage; new
    "stateless push" stage (retire the push state file —
    from the extraction review's "why does `Stage` live in
    a file?")
  - shared-doc-sync todos updated: vc-template-x1 synced
    2026-07-20; family grows to five (adds zc-msg-x1,
    tprobe)
  - version 0.73.0-0; backfill the 0.72.0-1 ref [57] in
    chores-13; open this file
- [[2]] 0.73.0-1 refactor: jj facade query module
  - new `src/jj.rs`: the `log(repo, rev, template)` primitive
    plus typed helpers — `matches` / `rev_exists`, `chid_of`,
    `cid_of` / `cid_short_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty` — every
    read-only `jj log -T` spawn now goes through it
  - folded: `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
    rev_is_empty_undescribed}` (the last two deleted, the
    empty-undescribed check rebuilt on `is_empty` +
    `desc_of`), `push::{get_change_id, jj_log_empty}`, the
    sanity verifiers' inline template blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
    `sync::{commit_id, revset_nonempty}` + the
    `commit_ids_of_bookmark` template, and
    `common::bookmark_publish_state`'s commit-id closure
  - `rev_exists` folds jj's unresolvable-revision errors to
    `false` (the `try_commit_id` patterns); the push
    stale-state checks keep their any-failure-is-stale
    behavior via `.unwrap_or(false)` at the call sites
- [[3]] 0.73.0-2 refactor: jj facade tracking parse
  - the two tracking parsers unify on one listing:
    `jj::bookmark_list` / `bookmark_list_all` join the
    facade, and `common::find_tracked_remote` (positive:
    indented `@remote` entry, synced or divergent form)
    sits beside `find_non_tracking_remote` (negative:
    column-0 ref) over the same `-a` output
  - `main::bm_track_one` drops its raw
    `std::process::Command` spawn + private parse — now a
    thin wrapper; `verify_tracking` and squash-push's
    bookmark-existence check use the facade
  - five `find_tracked_remote` unit tests
  - rider: TODO.md In Progress ladder rungs now carry
    prepended `[[N]]` commit refs (backfilled once pushed,
    like the chores As-built rungs); intro template updated
- [[4]] 0.73.0-3 refactor: jj facade ochid parse
  - `desc_helpers::extract_ochids` (column-0 trailers, all,
    in order, values trimmed) becomes the crate's one
    string-level ochid parser; `extract_ochid_from_desc` is
    now `.pop()` over it (the *last* trailer — matching
    `common::extract_ochid`'s previous reverse scan) and
    `common::extract_ochid` wraps that over
    `commit.description()`
  - `squash_push`'s local `extract_ochids` deleted; its
    three parser tests move to desc_helpers, plus a new
    multi-ochid last-wins test
  - behavior tightened: the single-value accessors are now
    column-0-only — an indented `ochid:` mention is prose,
    not a trailer (previously the trimmed scans matched it)
- [[5]] 0.73.0-4 test: jj facade fixture helpers
  - `test_helpers::jj_ok` (which anticipated this migration
    in its doc note) gains `cid` / `chid` / `description`;
    the near-identical private copies in
    `push/integration_tests.rs` and
    `sync/integration_tests.rs` are deleted (push keeps a
    local `desc_first_line` built on `jj_ok`)
  - deviation from the plan bullet: `tests/cli_sync.rs`
    lives in its own test crate and can't reach the
    binary's `test_helpers`, and its variant needs `HOME`
    injection — its `jj` / `cid` move to the crate's shared
    `tests/common/mod.rs` instead, with the why documented
  - the shared helpers spawn directly rather than calling
    `crate::jj`, so test inspection stays independent of
    the facade under test
- [[6]] 0.73.0 refactor: DRY jj facade (close-out)
  - ARCHITECTURE.md: `src/jj.rs` joins the module
    inventory; the stale subcommand enumeration corrected
    (`finalize` dropped; `squash_push`, `revert`,
    `validate_bot`, `config_cmd`, `bot_session` /
    `transcript`, `sync/state` added)
  - per-stage refactor Todos: each remaining program stage
    is a short ranked entry pointing at its stage section
    (por → dual to todo-backlog); the umbrella entry
    shrinks to the program pointer
  - `## Done` entry; `## In Progress` retired; ladder
    commit refs pruned (the As-built ladder here is the
    permanent record)

### Outcome

Every read-only jj query spawn now goes through `src/jj.rs`
(log templates + bookmark listings), with one tracking
parser pair and one ochid trailer parser beside it, and one
jj test helper per crate. The remaining `run(` sites are
mutations (commit / describe / bookmark / push / fetch /
squash / rebase / op), the git/gh spawns owned by the
de-gitify-init stage and the by-design `gh` calls, push's
`jj diff --stat` display passthrough, and the deliberate
direct spawns in test harnesses — the surface the jj-lib
migration stage inherits.

content.text:
Work complete and validated. Please review.

**What changed** (working-copy diff on top of the 0.73.0 `refactor-vc-x1` tip, 7 files):

- **AGENTS.md** — the `CargoRust.toml` mention now uses `vc-x1-work-repo-template`, wrapped exactly as the template's copy; the shared doc set is byte-identical again (verified with `diff`).
- **README.md** — the `init --use-template` examples: the renamed templates no longer satisfy the `<CODE>.claude` sibling default, so the real-template example now passes an explicit `CODE,BOT` pair, and a generic `../tmpl` example keeps the default behavior documented.
- **TODO.md** — the four old-name mentions in the two shared-doc-sync Todo entries renamed (the two `## Done` mentions kept as historical record); new `## Done` entry for this sweep with ref `[9]` (the free slot between `[8]` and `[10]`). `vc-x1 validate-todo` passes.
- **notes/todo-backlog.md** — entry 32 (restructure templates) renamed to the new pair.
- **notes/chores/chores-14.md** — new section `## docs: adopt new template repo names` with an empty `Commits:` line. While inserting it I noticed the file's trailing refs had no `# References` header (the convention requires one), so I added it — flag if you'd rather that ride separately.
- **Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock** — version 0.73.0 → 0.73.1.

Left alone deliberately: `notes/notes-sync-20260716.md`, `jj-tips.md`, chores history (historical records), and `src/init/tests.rs` (fixture directory names, not repo references).

Validation: `cargo fmt`, `clippy -D warnings`, full test suite (445+ tests, all green), `cargo install` — `vc-x1 -V` reports 0.73.1.

Proposed description (ochid injected by `vc-x1 push`, not hand-written):

**Title:** `docs: adopt new template repo names`

**Body:**
```
The template repos were renamed (vc-template-x1 →
vc-x1-work-repo-template, vc-template-x1.claude →
vc-x1-bot-repo-template); the template side adopted the new
names 2026-07-21. Sweep vc-x1's live mentions; historical
records keep the old name.

- AGENTS.md: CargoRust.toml mention uses the new name —
  byte-identical with the template's copy again
- README.md: init --use-template examples pass an explicit
  CODE,BOT pair (the new names don't satisfy the
  `<CODE>.claude` sibling default); a generic ../tmpl
  example keeps the default documented
- TODO.md: shared-doc-sync Todo entries renamed; Done
  entry + [9] ref for this sweep
- notes/todo-backlog.md: entry 32 (restructure templates)
  renamed
- notes/chores/chores-14.md: new section (empty Commits:);
  missing References header added above the refs
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.73.1
```

Note the mechanical renames were done by a Sonnet subagent per your delegation note (I corrected its AGENTS.md rewrap to byte-match the template). This commit would sit on the unpushed `refactor-vc-x1` line — where to push, and whether `main` stays parked, is a separate decision at push time.

input.description: Check push flags and bookmark handling

content.content:
vc-x1 0.73.1

Commit both repos, push the work repo's BOOKMARK, and
squash-push the bot repo's `main` — one resumable command.

Collapses the manual commit-push-publish ceremony into a
single subcommand with two interactive approval gates and a
state machine with persistent progress so interruptions can
resume without re-doing completed stages.

Stages: preflight (tracking / bot-published / sync checks —
no build steps; run project checks yourself before pushing)
→ review (approve diff)
→ message ($EDITOR / --title+--body, approve text) →
commit-work → commit-bot (skipped if clean) → bookmark-set
(work repo → <bookmark>, bot repo → main) → push-work →
squash-push-bot. Failures in commit-work / commit-bot /
bookmark-set roll both repos back via
`jj op restore` to the snapshot recorded before commit-work.
After push-work succeeds the remote boundary is crossed and
recovery is forward-only.

Non-interactive use: pass both --title and --body plus --yes
to skip the review gate. Saved state carries title/body
across resumes so only the first invocation needs them.

Usage: vc-x1 push [OPTIONS] [BOOKMARK]

Arguments:
  [BOOKMARK]
          Bookmark to advance in the work repo (positional form of
          `--bookmark`). The bot repo always advances `main`.
          
          Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`
          without the `--bookmark` ceremony; `--bookmark` is kept as an alias
          for scripts and for composition with other args. The two forms
          conflict if both supplied.

Options:
      --bookmark <BOOKMARK>
          Bookmark to advance in the work repo (flag form; see positional)

  -V, --version
          Print the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as the first line, then continue. With
          no subcommand, prints the banner and exits.
          
          Replaces clap's auto-version (which would exit after printing): the
          banner now rides along with normal subcommand execution rather than
          gating it, so scripts can capture the version *and* the command's
          output in one invocation.

      --restart
          Clear any saved state file and start from stage 1

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --from <STAGE>
          Explicit stage to jump to (advanced / debug use)

          Possible values:
          - preflight:       Verify bookmark tracking, the bot-published
            invariant, and sync state
          - review:          Present diff for the first approval gate
          - message:         Compose / edit the commit message; present for
            second gate
          - commit-work:     Commit the work repo
          - commit-bot:      Commit the `.claude` bot repo (skipped if empty)
          - bookmark-set:    Advance each repo's bookmark to its `@-`: work →
            `<bookmark>`, bot → `main`
          - push-work:       `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`
          - squash-push-bot: In-process squash of `.claude`'s trailing session
            writes + push `main`

      --log <LOG>
          Log file path (captures all levels)

      --step
          Pause between every stage for an interactive approval gate

      --status
          Print where the saved state thinks we are and exit

      --recheck
          Re-run `preflight` even on resume (default: skip if last run
          succeeded)

      --no-squash-push
          Stop before `squash-push-bot` so it can be run manually

      --dry-run
          Print the exact commands for every stage without side effects

      --title <STR>
          Commit title (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

      --body <STR>
          Commit body (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

  -y, --yes
          Auto-approve interactive prompts (review gate, message-edit
          confirmation). Required when `--title` / `--body` aren't both supplied
          in non-interactive contexts

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Chores-14

Continuation of `chores-13.md` (closed at `0.72.x`, parked on
the `support-trapezoid-commits` bookmark). This file covers
the jj refactor program
([refactor-20260716.md](../refactor-20260716.md)) from
`0.73.0` onward, worked on the `refactor-vc-x1` bookmark
while `main` parks at the `0.71.0` tip.

Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`AGENTS.md`](../../AGENTS.md#reference-numbering); chores-14
starts at `[1]`.

## refactor: DRY jj facade

Commits: see [As-built ladder](#as-built-ladder)

~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,
"--no-graph", "-T", <template>, "-R", <repo>])` and each
module has quietly grown a private wrapper — spotted at
0.69.0-2, where `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind"
but was the Nth reinvention. One typed facade module ends
the reinvention; first stage of the refactor program.

### As-built ladder

- [[1]] 0.73.0-0 chore: open jj facade cycle
  - restore the five notes files from
    `support-trapezoid-commits` (TODO.md, todo-backlog,
    forks-multi-user, refactor doc, chores-13) so the shared
    bookkeeping rides the refactor line
  - re-plan after the pivot: `## In Progress` → the DRY
    facade ladder; the trapezoid `--merge [<base>]` design
    folded into the refactor doc's trapezoid stage; new
    "stateless push" stage (retire the push state file —
    from the extraction review's "why does `Stage` live in
    a file?")
  - shared-doc-sync todos updated: vc-template-x1 synced
    2026-07-20; family grows to five (adds zc-msg-x1,
    tprobe)
  - version 0.73.0-0; backfill the 0.72.0-1 ref [57] in
    chores-13; open this file
- [[2]] 0.73.0-1 refactor: jj facade query module
  - new `src/jj.rs`: the `log(repo, rev, template)` primitive
    plus typed helpers — `matches` / `rev_exists`, `chid_of`,
    `cid_of` / `cid_short_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty` — every
    read-only `jj log -T` spawn now goes through it
  - folded: `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
    rev_is_empty_undescribed}` (the last two deleted, the
    empty-undescribed check rebuilt on `is_empty` +
    `desc_of`), `push::{get_change_id, jj_log_empty}`, the
    sanity verifiers' inline template blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
    `sync::{commit_id, revset_nonempty}` + the
    `commit_ids_of_bookmark` template, and
    `common::bookmark_publish_state`'s commit-id closure
  - `rev_exists` folds jj's unresolvable-revision errors to
    `false` (the `try_commit_id` patterns); the push
    stale-state checks keep their any-failure-is-stale
    behavior via `.unwrap_or(false)` at the call sites
- [[3]] 0.73.0-2 refactor: jj facade tracking parse
  - the two tracking parsers unify on one listing:
    `jj::bookmark_list` / `bookmark_list_all` join the
    facade, and `common::find_tracked_remote` (positive:
    indented `@remote` entry, synced or divergent form)
    sits beside `find_non_tracking_remote` (negative:
    column-0 ref) over the same `-a` output
  - `main::bm_track_one` drops its raw
    `std::process::Command` spawn + private parse — now a
    thin wrapper; `verify_tracking` and squash-push's
    bookmark-existence check use the facade
  - five `find_tracked_remote` unit tests
  - rider: TODO.md In Progress ladder rungs now carry
    prepended `[[N]]` commit refs (backfilled once pushed,
    like the chores As-built rungs); intro template updated
- [[4]] 0.73.0-3 refactor: jj facade ochid parse
  - `desc_helpers::extract_ochids` (column-0 trailers, all,
    in order, values trimmed) becomes the crate's one
    string-level ochid parser; `extract_ochid_from_desc` is
    now `.pop()` over it (the *last* trailer — matching
    `common::extract_ochid`'s previous reverse scan) and
    `common::extract_ochid` wraps that over
    `commit.description()`
  - `squash_push`'s local `extract_ochids` deleted; its
    three parser tests move to desc_helpers, plus a new
    multi-ochid last-wins test
  - behavior tightened: the single-value accessors are now
    column-0-only — an indented `ochid:` mention is prose,
    not a trailer (previously the trimmed scans matched it)
- [[5]] 0.73.0-4 test: jj facade fixture helpers
  - `test_helpers::jj_ok` (which anticipated this migration
    in its doc note) gains `cid` / `chid` / `description`;
    the near-identical private copies in
    `push/integration_tests.rs` and
    `sync/integration_tests.rs` are deleted (push keeps a
    local `desc_first_line` built on `jj_ok`)
  - deviation from the plan bullet: `tests/cli_sync.rs`
    lives in its own test crate and can't reach the
    binary's `test_helpers`, and its variant needs `HOME`
    injection — its `jj` / `cid` move to the crate's shared
    `tests/common/mod.rs` instead, with the why documented
  - the shared helpers spawn directly rather than calling
    `crate::jj`, so test inspection stays independent of
    the facade under test
- [[6]] 0.73.0 refactor: DRY jj facade (close-out)
  - ARCHITECTURE.md: `src/jj.rs` joins the module
    inventory; the stale subcommand enumeration corrected
    (`finalize` dropped; `squash_push`, `revert`,
    `validate_bot`, `config_cmd`, `bot_session` /
    `transcript`, `sync/state` added)
  - per-stage refactor Todos: each remaining program stage
    is a short ranked entry pointing at its stage section
    (por → dual to todo-backlog); the umbrella entry
    shrinks to the program pointer
  - `## Done` entry; `## In Progress` retired; ladder
    commit refs pruned (the As-built ladder here is the
    permanent record)

### Outcome

Every read-only jj query spawn now goes through `src/jj.rs`
(log templates + bookmark listings), with one tracking
parser pair and one ochid trailer parser beside it, and one
jj test helper per crate. The remaining `run(` sites are
mutations (commit / describe / bookmark / push / fetch /
squash / rebase / op), the git/gh spawns owned by the
de-gitify-init stage and the by-design `gh` calls, push's
`jj diff --stat` display passthrough, and the deliberate
direct spawns in test harnesses — the surface the jj-lib
migration stage inherits.

## docs: adopt new template repo names

Commits:

The template repos were renamed (`vc-template-x1` →
`vc-x1-work-repo-template`, `vc-template-x1.claude` →
`vc-x1-bot-repo-template`); the template side adopted the new
names 2026-07-21. Sweep vc-x1's live mentions to match and
restore shared-doc-set byte-identity.

- AGENTS.md re-syncs byte-identical with the template's copy
  (the `CargoRust.toml` mention was the one divergent hunk)
- README.md init examples: the real templates no longer
  satisfy the `<CODE>.claude` sibling default, so the sweep
  makes that example an explicit `CODE,BOT` pair; a generic
  `../tmpl` example keeps the default documented
- Historical records keep the old name (`## Done` entries,
  the notes-sync manifest, chores, jj-tips) — GitHub
  redirects renamed repos. `src/init/tests.rs` fixture names
  are arbitrary strings, not repo references, and stay.

# References

message.content: So while I've been away talking with you.vc-x1-work-repo-template we've changed some things, please take a look and express your opinion, push back loudly if we've stepped on your toes :)

attachment.snippet:
1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder".
15	Each rung is prepended with its commit reference — a literal
16	`[[N]]` placeholder until the commit is pushed, then backfilled
17	to a real file-local `[[n]]` ref (same pattern as the chores
18	As-built rungs):
19	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
20	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
21	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
22	   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation
23	
24	_No cycle currently in progress._
25	
26	## Todo
27	
28	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
29	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
30	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
31	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
32	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
33	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
34	 number. Long-tail entries
35	 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
36	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
37	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
38	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
39	
40	1. **Refactor: typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process; end
41	   subprocess spawning.** Version-control operations are
42	   ~30 hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns plus every
43	   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
44	   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
45	   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
46	   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](notes/bugs.md))
47	   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
48	   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
49	   former Todos live in
50	   [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md).
51	   - The remaining stages are the individual
52	     "Refactor stage: …" entries below (listed in program
53	     order; the doc owns execution order, per-stage status,
54	     and design — DRY facade shipped at 0.73.0).
55	2. **Refactor stage: hygiene riders.** Terminology
56	   stragglers + single-field `options_flags` leaves — sweeps
57	   that churn the lines later stages rewrite; see
58	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-hygiene-riders).
59	   Runs next, after the DRY facade.
60	3. **Refactor stage: facade owns topology.** Repo resolution
61	   becomes facade state — `validate-desc`/`fix-desc` por
62	   equalization, configurable bot-repo dir; see
63	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-facade-owns-topology).
64	   After hygiene riders.
65	4. **Refactor stage: de-gitify init.** Replace init's
66	   strip-jj → git-push → re-colocate flow with the verified
67	   jj-only sequence; see
68	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-de-gitify-init).
69	   After facade owns topology.
70	5. **Refactor stage: split push.rs.** Extract `push/state.rs`
71	   so the jj-lib migration reviews cleanly — built and parked
72	   on `support-trapezoid-commits` (0.72.0-1), replay or redo;
73	   see
74	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-split-pushrs).
75	6. **Refactor stage: stateless push.** Retire the push state
76	   file — derive resume from repo reality; see
77	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-stateless-push).
78	   After split push.rs.
79	7. **Refactor stage: jj-lib migration.** Facade internals and
80	   mutations move in-process; the index-lock retry becomes
81	   ours; see
82	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-jj-lib-migration).
83	   After split push.rs and stateless push.
84	8. **Refactor stage: push body-intro validation.** Validate
85	   the commit body opens with a non-dash intro line, with a
86	   clear error; see
87	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-push-body-intro-validation).
88	   After the jj-lib migration (program order).
89	9. **Refactor stage: trapezoid close-out.** `push --merge
90	   [<base>]` — the native trapezoid close-out; design settled
91	   in the stage notes; see
92	   [the stage](notes/refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out).
93	   After stateless push (no state-file growth) and jj-lib.
94	10. **Restructure templates: single template repo + fixed bot
95	    seed manifest.** Replace the separate
96	    `vc-x1-work-repo-template` + `vc-x1-bot-repo-template`
97	    repos with the one work-repo template, whose live
98	    `.claude/` doubles as the bot-side seed source; retire
99	    `vc-x1-bot-repo-template`. `vc-x1 init` / `clone` updates
100	    for the new layout. First up after the refactor program.
101	    - `--use-template` rule: explicit `CODE,BOT` copies all
102	      non-hidden files from BOT (unchanged — the escape
103	      hatch for rich bot seeds); `CODE` alone seeds the bot
104	      side from a fixed manifest — `LICENSE-*`, `README.md`
105	      — taken from `<CODE>/.claude/`. The `<CODE>.claude`
106	      sibling default is dropped.
107	    - The manifest is the safety property: a live `.claude`
108	      has non-hidden session artifacts at top level, and
109	      the known subset is what lets it double as the seed
110	      source without leaking session history into new
111	      projects.
112	    - Manifest members missing in the source are skipped —
113	      a code template with no `.claude/` content yields a
114	      bare-but-valid bot repo (the bot template is
115	      optional; init already generates the true minimum
116	      itself).
117	    - `memory/MEMORY.md` moves from copied to generated:
118	      it is intentionally empty (seeded only because Claude
119	      tends to create it otherwise), so init emits it like
120	      `.vc-config.toml` instead of copying — no "is it
121	      still empty?" invariant left in the template.
122	11. **ochid: bot-repo location qualifier.** An ochid is
123	    workspace-relative (`/.claude/<chid>`) — nothing in a
124	    published commit says *where* the companion bot repo
125	    lives (vc-x1's is `github.com/winksaville/vc-x1.claude`,
126	    discoverable only by convention). Anyone cloning just the
127	    work repo can't resolve bot-side ochids. Design already
128	    sketched in forks-multi-user.md
129	    [Per-user bot repos via URL-shaped ochid](notes/forks-multi-user.md#per-user-bot-repos-via-url-shaped-ochid):
130	    URL-shaped trailers, plus the complementary
131	    `.vc-config.toml` repo-index form; resolver dispatch is
132	    one rule (URL → fetch, else workspace-relative), existing
133	    path-form trailers stay the backward-compatible case.
134	    - Cheap first rung: declare the companion's URL once in
135	      the committed `.vc-config.toml` (no trailer-format
136	      change; any work-repo clone then knows where the bot
137	      repo lives). Rides naturally with the refactor
138	      program's facade-owns-topology stage
139	      (bot-repo-location config).
140	    - Link rot + mirroring mitigations are in the same doc
141	      section.
142	12. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
143	    baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
144	    change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
145	    version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
146	    `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
147	    while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
148	    But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
149	    merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
150	    ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
151	    so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
152	    work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
153	    therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
154	    workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
155	    the cycle after 0.65.2.

content.text:
Verdict first: the mechanics are clean, but I do want to push back on the substance — you've reversed a decision that was committed fourteen hours ago, and one of the two files you touched breaks the byte-identity we just finished restoring.

What I found: the template's working copy (uncommitted, on top of `tkumntov`) renames `vc-template-x1` → `vc-x1-work-repo-template` in the two **historical** files — `notes/chores/chores-01.md` (the "byte-for-byte across …" sentence plus the two commit-URL refs) and `notes/jj-tips.md` (the `jj git remote add` example and every recorded prompt path in the two captured transcripts). The edits themselves are tidy: no botched replacements (my first read of the default jj diff format fooled me — the `--git` diff shows clean swaps), no trailing whitespace introduced, sensible rewrap, and the renamed commit URLs still resolve since GitHub serves old-name redirects both ways.

Now the push-back, in descending loudness:

1. **This contradicts the immediately preceding commit's own rationale.** `tkumntov`'s body says, verbatim: "Historical files (notes/chores/chores-01.md, notes/jj-tips.md) keep the old name; GitHub redirects renamed repos." These edits rename exactly those two files, one commit later. Changing your mind is fine — but the record shouldn't contradict itself silently. If this lands, the commit body must explicitly supersede that note, or a future reader (me, next session) will treat the older statement as still-operative policy. I'd also note both my vc-x1 sweep commit and its chores section say "historical records keep the old name" on the strength of that rule.

2. **`jj-tips.md` is in the byte-identical shared doc set.** The sentence being edited in chores-01 says so itself. I pushed vc-x1's sweep a few hours ago with "shared doc set byte-identical again" as its headline; this edit forks `jj-tips.md` template-side again. If it lands, the family (vc-x1, iiac-perf, zc-msg-x1, tprobe) owes a jj-tips re-sync — that's a new entry in vc-x1's shared-doc-sync Todo family, not a free edit.

3. **Rewriting captured transcripts falsifies the record.** The jj-tips examples are real terminal captures from 2026-03-13, when the repo *was* `vc-template-x1` — the prompt paths are facts of that capture. This is the same measured-vs-inferred instinct behind the "We think" convention: don't make history claim things that weren't so. That said, there's a fair counterargument I'll state plainly: in a template repo, those transcripts function as pedagogy, not archive — a fresh reader sees consistent naming throughout, which is arguably the template's whole job. If that's the reasoning, it justifies jj-tips but *not* chores-01, which is pure historical narrative ("the sync happened across these repos") and gains nothing from the rename.

My recommendation: `jj restore` the chores-01 change (keep history as it happened), and make a deliberate call on jj-tips — either restore it too and keep `tkumntov`'s rule intact, or keep the rename with a commit body that supersedes the old rationale *and* add the family-wide jj-tips re-sync Todo in vc-x1. What I'd push back on hardest is landing both as a silent "renames, part 2" — the halfway state where the record says one policy and the files show another.

The plain version: your changes are technically fine and nothing of mine broke — but the previous commit promised these two files would keep the old name, one of them is supposed to stay byte-identical across five repos, and rewriting old terminal captures makes history lie a little. Keep the chores file as it was; for the tips file, either revert or own the reversal explicitly and plan the cross-repo re-sync.

Synced — vc-x1's `notes/jj-tips.md` is now byte-identical with the template's working-copy version. It sits uncommitted in vc-x1's `@` alongside your own TODO.md / refactor-20260716.md / todo-backlog.md edits, so it'll ride in whatever commit comes next; nothing more is needed even after you commit the template side, since both copies already match.

And yes, the vision makes sense — it's the logical endpoint of a principle already in the conventions. The current rule ("git owns the mechanical record; chores owns the narrative") exists because the *real* narrative — the session itself — isn't presentable yet. Chores is a hand-written distillation of a conversation that's already recorded verbatim in the bot repo, and hand-written copies drift; that's the same DRY argument that removed edit lists from chores in the first place. Once vc-x1 can render commit ↔ session through the ochid links the way you imagine, the distillation becomes redundant and chores collapses into what you said: an index.

Two observations about that endgame worth keeping in view:

- The ochid trailer already links a work commit to a bot-repo *commit*, but a session commit can span hours of conversation. What an index adds is within-session granularity — which stretch of the session produced this commit, where the design argument happened. That index could plausibly be *generated* (the session transcript knows when pushes happened) rather than hand-written, which would make it drift-proof — the property chores never had.
- The one thing chores does today that isn't in the session verbatim is *curation*: the `###` design subsections that Todos link into as live references. Sessions are an immutable journal — great as record, poor as a thing to cite into. So some curated design layer probably survives (your `refactor-20260716.md` pattern is already this: a design doc that outlives any one cycle), while the per-cycle narrative sections are what go away.

The template call is consistent with all of this: a seeded project starts with zero chores history, and its real history accrues in its own commits and bot repo from day one.

If you want, I can capture this as an `## Ideas` entry in TODO.md ("chores → session index once bot-session presentation matures") so the direction is on the record — say the word, since you've got TODO.md open in flight yourself.

## Ideas

 Items not yet solid enough for `## Todo` (or surfaced
 during close-out / end-of-day before they're fully
 formed). Triaged at the next Preparation: promote to
 `## Todo` / `notes/todo-backlog.md`, fold into a
 picked-up cycle, or drop.

1. **`vc` as a code+conversation provenance tool (grander
   ambition).** Today `vc-x1` manages a dual repo (code +
   `.claude`) cross-linked by `ochid:`. The larger aim is
   to *surface* that link — view history with the
   conversation and the code side by side: provenance, the
   *why* of a change, not just the *what*. The dual-repo +
   `ochid` design is already the substrate; the cross-links
   make code↔conversation navigable, so the viewer is UI
   over an already-solved data link.
   - Build direction: keep resolution/assembly in `vc` — an
     editor-agnostic Rust engine/lib extending the
     `show` / `chid` / `desc` family ("given a commit,
     resolve its ochid and assemble the paired diff +
     conversation slice"); the editor add-on is a thin
     presentation layer over it.
   - Front-end leans a Zed add-on (Rust, preferred), maybe
     VSCode / other. Verify Zed's extension API can host a
     rich side-by-side panel before committing — an
     editor-agnostic core hedges the bet.
   - `vc-x2`? A rewrite is unwarranted: the audit's
     Commonality pass found the architecture sound (por is
     bolted on where an existing good pattern wasn't
     applied) — equalize incrementally. "vc-x2" only makes
     sense if the viewer changes the *core* architecture
     (an index / daemon / data model). Separate
     engine-rewrite (no) from product-reposition (open).
   - Possible artifact: a top-level
     `notes/design-cli/vision.md` framing the direction,
     with the parity and conversion docs as sub-designs.
2. **Restructure the design-cli parity docs (target
   0.63.0).** `por-dual-parity-audit.md` (~1200 lines)
   fuses a *frozen* audit (the `## 1`–`## 8` snapshot
   evidence) with a *living* design (axes, decisions,
   matrix, gap list); the "audit" name undersells it and
   the halves have different lifecycles. And
   `por-dual-parity.md` (the stub) overlaps on parity but
   uniquely holds the `por ↔ dual` conversion design.
   - Split the audit doc into a frozen audit snapshot + a
     living design doc (names TBD; could reclaim
     `por-dual-parity.md` for the design).
   - Refocus the stub to conversion-only and rename (e.g.
     `por-dual-conversion.md`); drop its redundant parity
     half.
   - Repoint refs (`todo.md` `[1]` + the `por → dual` Todo,
     `copying.md`, the audit's internal anchors + Reading
     guide) and validate; `chores-10/11/12` mentions are
     historical and stay.
   - Promote the Gap-list items to anchored
     `#### Gap N — <title>` sub-headings so cross-cycle
     citations can deep-link a specific gap (markdown
     anchors headings, not list items). Trade-off: stable
     anchors, but the ordinal lives in the heading text
     (manual renumber on reorder) — fine for a consumed
     backlog. The 3 `Gap #N` links in the `0.62.0`
     close-out chores narrative resolve only to the section
     until this lands.
   - Deferred from the 0.62.0 close-out: close-out is
     bookkeeping-only, and the split is substantive,
     anchor-heavy work warranting its own cycle.

## Bugs

   and code side by side, the hand-written chores narrative
   is a distillation of a conversation the bot repo already
   records verbatim — the DRY argument that removed edit
   lists from chores (git owns the mechanics) then applies
   to the narrative too (the session owns it). Chores
   collapses to an index into the session.
   - The `ochid:` trailer links a work commit to a session
     *commit*; the index adds within-session granularity —
     which conversation span produced the commit, where the
     design argument happened. We think it can be generated
     (the transcript records when pushes happen), making it
     drift-proof where hand-written chores never were.
   - What survives: the curated design layer (the
     refactor-20260716.md pattern). Sessions are an
     immutable journal — good as record, poor to cite
     into — so live design references keep pointing at
     curated docs, not per-cycle narrative sections.
   - The template side already points this way: chores
     files are not seeded; a new project's history is its
     own commits + bot session from day one.

+     design argument happened. We think it can be generated
+     (the transcript records when pushes happen), making it
+     drift-proof where hand-written chores never were.
+   - What survives: the curated design layer (the
+     immutable journal — good as record, poor to cite
+     into — so live design references keep pointing at
+     curated docs, not per-cycle narrative sections.
+   - The template side already points this way: chores
+     files are not seeded; a new project's history is its
+     own commits + bot session from day one.

-## docs: shared protocol set; template cleanup
-
-Commits: [[2]]
-
-Two threads land together. First, the notes-sync round with
-vc-x1: the shared protocol set — `AGENTS.md`,
-`notes/cycle-protocol.md`, `notes/versioning.md`,
-`notes/jj-tips.md` — is now byte-identical across the sibling
-projects (iiac-perf receives the files after this round).
-Second, template cleanup: strip template-inherited scaffolding
-so a cloned repo starts clean, and land the Rust unwrap policy.
-
-- Repo terminology standardized: **work repo** / **bot repo**,
-  written as two words, hyphenated only when the pair sits
-  directly in front of another noun. Defined once in AGENTS.md
-  Project Structure; all variants (app repo, session repo,
-  code-side, `.claude` repo) swept from the living docs.
-- vc-x1 0.69.0 corrections folded in from the sync round:
-  `squash-push` replaces the detached `finalize` flow, push
-  preflight is repo-state-only (the medium's validation is the
-  per-commit flow's job), and the hard stop follows the user's
-  directive rather than every push.
-- Sections adopted from siblings: "Plain synopsis after
-  technical explanations" (from iiac-perf); the lean-not-ban
-  `unwrap*`/`expect` policy with project-wide clippy lints,
-  seeded for new Rust projects via `CargoRust.toml`.
-- `notes/substep-test.sh` removed — the jj squash-ladder
-  scratch harness that validated the sub-cycle squash recipes.
-  Recover it from its introducing commit [[1]]:
-  `git show bd2902617c2f:notes/substep-test.sh`.
-
-## docs: converge shared protocol doc set
-
-Commits:

-template takes the four review fixes agreed across the three
-sessions; the adopters replaced their older copies wholesale, so
-per-repo diffs differ while the resulting files are identical.
-(`notes/versioning.md`, the set's fourth file, was untouched
-this round and remains identical everywhere.)
-
-- The commit body carries a sha256 manifest (12-hex) of the
-  three converged files, so any repo can verify convergence
-  later without cross-repo diffs.
-- Review fixes: a stale "clippy runs in preflight" claim in
-  AGENTS.md — written before preflight became repo-state-only —
-  and three typo-level fixes in jj-tips.md's force-push example.

+18. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Chores-14

Continuation of `chores-13.md` (closed at `0.72.x`, parked on
the `support-trapezoid-commits` bookmark). This file covers
the jj refactor program
([refactor-20260716.md](../refactor-20260716.md)) from
`0.73.0` onward, worked on the `refactor-vc-x1` bookmark
while `main` parks at the `0.71.0` tip.

Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`AGENTS.md`](../../AGENTS.md#reference-numbering); chores-14
starts at `[1]`.

## refactor: DRY jj facade

Commits: see [As-built ladder](#as-built-ladder)

~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,
"--no-graph", "-T", <template>, "-R", <repo>])` and each
module has quietly grown a private wrapper — spotted at
0.69.0-2, where `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind"
but was the Nth reinvention. One typed facade module ends
the reinvention; first stage of the refactor program.

### As-built ladder

- [[1]] 0.73.0-0 chore: open jj facade cycle
  - restore the five notes files from
    `support-trapezoid-commits` (TODO.md, todo-backlog,
    forks-multi-user, refactor doc, chores-13) so the shared
    bookkeeping rides the refactor line
  - re-plan after the pivot: `## In Progress` → the DRY
    facade ladder; the trapezoid `--merge [<base>]` design
    folded into the refactor doc's trapezoid stage; new
    "stateless push" stage (retire the push state file —
    from the extraction review's "why does `Stage` live in
    a file?")
  - shared-doc-sync todos updated: vc-template-x1 synced
    2026-07-20; family grows to five (adds zc-msg-x1,
    tprobe)
  - version 0.73.0-0; backfill the 0.72.0-1 ref [57] in
    chores-13; open this file
- [[2]] 0.73.0-1 refactor: jj facade query module
  - new `src/jj.rs`: the `log(repo, rev, template)` primitive
    plus typed helpers — `matches` / `rev_exists`, `chid_of`,
    `cid_of` / `cid_short_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty` — every
    read-only `jj log -T` spawn now goes through it
  - folded: `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
    rev_is_empty_undescribed}` (the last two deleted, the
    empty-undescribed check rebuilt on `is_empty` +
    `desc_of`), `push::{get_change_id, jj_log_empty}`, the
    sanity verifiers' inline template blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
    `sync::{commit_id, revset_nonempty}` + the
    `commit_ids_of_bookmark` template, and
    `common::bookmark_publish_state`'s commit-id closure
  - `rev_exists` folds jj's unresolvable-revision errors to
    `false` (the `try_commit_id` patterns); the push
    stale-state checks keep their any-failure-is-stale
    behavior via `.unwrap_or(false)` at the call sites
- [[3]] 0.73.0-2 refactor: jj facade tracking parse
  - the two tracking parsers unify on one listing:
    `jj::bookmark_list` / `bookmark_list_all` join the
    facade, and `common::find_tracked_remote` (positive:
    indented `@remote` entry, synced or divergent form)
    sits beside `find_non_tracking_remote` (negative:
    column-0 ref) over the same `-a` output
  - `main::bm_track_one` drops its raw
    `std::process::Command` spawn + private parse — now a
    thin wrapper; `verify_tracking` and squash-push's
    bookmark-existence check use the facade
  - five `find_tracked_remote` unit tests
  - rider: TODO.md In Progress ladder rungs now carry
    prepended `[[N]]` commit refs (backfilled once pushed,
    like the chores As-built rungs); intro template updated
- [[4]] 0.73.0-3 refactor: jj facade ochid parse
  - `desc_helpers::extract_ochids` (column-0 trailers, all,
    in order, values trimmed) becomes the crate's one
    string-level ochid parser; `extract_ochid_from_desc` is
    now `.pop()` over it (the *last* trailer — matching
    `common::extract_ochid`'s previous reverse scan) and
    `common::extract_ochid` wraps that over
    `commit.description()`
  - `squash_push`'s local `extract_ochids` deleted; its
    three parser tests move to desc_helpers, plus a new
    multi-ochid last-wins test
  - behavior tightened: the single-value accessors are now
    column-0-only — an indented `ochid:` mention is prose,
    not a trailer (previously the trimmed scans matched it)
- [[5]] 0.73.0-4 test: jj facade fixture helpers
  - `test_helpers::jj_ok` (which anticipated this migration
    in its doc note) gains `cid` / `chid` / `description`;
    the near-identical private copies in
    `push/integration_tests.rs` and
    `sync/integration_tests.rs` are deleted (push keeps a
    local `desc_first_line` built on `jj_ok`)
  - deviation from the plan bullet: `tests/cli_sync.rs`
    lives in its own test crate and can't reach the
    binary's `test_helpers`, and its variant needs `HOME`
    injection — its `jj` / `cid` move to the crate's shared
    `tests/common/mod.rs` instead, with the why documented
  - the shared helpers spawn directly rather than calling
    `crate::jj`, so test inspection stays independent of
    the facade under test
- [[6]] 0.73.0 refactor: DRY jj facade (close-out)
  - ARCHITECTURE.md: `src/jj.rs` joins the module
    inventory; the stale subcommand enumeration corrected
    (`finalize` dropped; `squash_push`, `revert`,
    `validate_bot`, `config_cmd`, `bot_session` /
    `transcript`, `sync/state` added)
  - per-stage refactor Todos: each remaining program stage
    is a short ranked entry pointing at its stage section
    (por → dual to todo-backlog); the umbrella entry
    shrinks to the program pointer
  - `## Done` entry; `## In Progress` retired; ladder
    commit refs pruned (the As-built ladder here is the
    permanent record)

### Outcome

Every read-only jj query spawn now goes through `src/jj.rs`
(log templates + bookmark listings), with one tracking
parser pair and one ochid trailer parser beside it, and one
jj test helper per crate. The remaining `run(` sites are
mutations (commit / describe / bookmark / push / fetch /
squash / rebase / op), the git/gh spawns owned by the
de-gitify-init stage and the by-design `gh` calls, push's
`jj diff --stat` display passthrough, and the deliberate
direct spawns in test harnesses — the surface the jj-lib
migration stage inherits.

## docs: adopt new template repo names

Commits:

The template repos were renamed (`vc-template-x1` →
`vc-x1-work-repo-template`, `vc-template-x1.claude` →
`vc-x1-bot-repo-template`); the template side adopted the new
names 2026-07-21. Sweep vc-x1's live mentions to match and
restore shared-doc-set byte-identity.

- AGENTS.md re-syncs byte-identical with the template's copy
  (the `CargoRust.toml` mention was the one divergent hunk)
- README.md init examples: the real templates no longer
  satisfy the `<CODE>.claude` sibling default, so the sweep
  makes that example an explicit `CODE,BOT` pair; a generic
  `../tmpl` example keeps the default documented
- Historical records keep the old name (`## Done` entries,
  the notes-sync manifest, chores, jj-tips) — GitHub
  redirects renamed repos. `src/init/tests.rs` fixture names
  are arbitrary strings, not repo references, and stay.

# References

[1]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/f761e89092df "f761e89092dfbb82e8ab355d6e5a058e77b07e23"
[2]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/47e5075b90da "47e5075b90daa5e9b24fa7c93a5814a2eee0f03f"
[3]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/5a61ebdcbac8 "5a61ebdcbac872eac03d6b70141030217be1f850"
[4]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/c3a6d258f511 "c3a6d258f511ae4a3a6f0b6e42aba80d5005d4e8"
[5]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/303d163196ab "303d163196ab4c387428e4bec0fc65430ead4206"
[6]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/6c93a011a54d "6c93a011a54dd990035733e49f0bfa169ebad609"

## docs: notes rework + config refresh

Commits:

Alignment batch riding the template-side session: vc-x1
adopts what the working pair (vc-x1 + the template) decided.

- `.vc-config.toml` (both sides) now carries init's
  generated optional-keys block, so the hand-seeded configs
  are byte-identical with what `vc-x1 init` emits (the block
  is schema-sourced and cannot drift from the binary).
- jj-tips.md re-syncs with the template under a
  reclassification: its recorded transcripts are pedagogy,
  not history, so example names adopt
  `vc-x1-work-repo-template`. This supersedes the previous
  section's "historical records keep the old name" for this
  one file; chores files and the notes-sync manifest remain
  historical and keep old names.
- The template-restructure design (single template repo +
  fixed bot seed manifest, `<CODE>.claude` sibling default
  dropped) is promoted to Todo #10; the `.bot` default-name
  decision and the symmetric config schema fold into the
  refactor program's facade-owns-topology stage.
- New Idea recorded: chores retire into a session index
  once the provenance viewer can present session + code
  (the template already seeds no chores history — a new
  project's history is its commits + bot session).

- [[2]] 0.73.0-1 refactor: jj facade query module
  - new `src/jj.rs`: the `log(repo, rev, template)` primitive
    plus typed helpers — `matches` / `rev_exists`, `chid_of`,
    `cid_of` / `cid_short_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty` — every
    read-only `jj log -T` spawn now goes through it
  - folded: `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
    rev_is_empty_undescribed}` (the last two deleted, the
    empty-undescribed check rebuilt on `is_empty` +
    `desc_of`), `push::{get_change_id, jj_log_empty}`, the
    sanity verifiers' inline template blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
    `sync::{commit_id, revset_nonempty}` + the
    `commit_ids_of_bookmark` template, and
    `common::bookmark_publish_state`'s commit-id closure
  - `rev_exists` folds jj's unresolvable-revision errors to
    `false` (the `try_commit_id` patterns); the push
    stale-state checks keep their any-failure-is-stale
    behavior via `.unwrap_or(false)` at the call sites
- [[3]] 0.73.0-2 refactor: jj facade tracking parse
  - the two tracking parsers unify on one listing:
    `jj::bookmark_list` / `bookmark_list_all` join the
    facade, and `common::find_tracked_remote` (positive:
    indented `@remote` entry, synced or divergent form)
    sits beside `find_non_tracking_remote` (negative:
    column-0 ref) over the same `-a` output
  - `main::bm_track_one` drops its raw
    `std::process::Command` spawn + private parse — now a
    thin wrapper; `verify_tracking` and squash-push's
    bookmark-existence check use the facade
  - five `find_tracked_remote` unit tests
  - rider: TODO.md In Progress ladder rungs now carry
    prepended `[[N]]` commit refs (backfilled once pushed,
    like the chores As-built rungs); intro template updated
- [[4]] 0.73.0-3 refactor: jj facade ochid parse
  - `desc_helpers::extract_ochids` (column-0 trailers, all,
    in order, values trimmed) becomes the crate's one
    string-level ochid parser; `extract_ochid_from_desc` is
    now `.pop()` over it (the *last* trailer — matching
    `common::extract_ochid`'s previous reverse scan) and
    `common::extract_ochid` wraps that over
    `commit.description()`
  - `squash_push`'s local `extract_ochids` deleted; its
    three parser tests move to desc_helpers, plus a new
    multi-ochid last-wins test
  - behavior tightened: the single-value accessors are now
    column-0-only — an indented `ochid:` mention is prose,
    not a trailer (previously the trimmed scans matched it)
- [[5]] 0.73.0-4 test: jj facade fixture helpers
  - `test_helpers::jj_ok` (which anticipated this migration
    in its doc note) gains `cid` / `chid` / `description`;
    the near-identical private copies in
    `push/integration_tests.rs` and
    `sync/integration_tests.rs` are deleted (push keeps a
    local `desc_first_line` built on `jj_ok`)
  - deviation from the plan bullet: `tests/cli_sync.rs`
    lives in its own test crate and can't reach the
    binary's `test_helpers`, and its variant needs `HOME`
    injection — its `jj` / `cid` move to the crate's shared
    `tests/common/mod.rs` instead, with the why documented
  - the shared helpers spawn directly rather than calling
    `crate::jj`, so test inspection stays independent of
    the facade under test
- [[6]] 0.73.0 refactor: DRY jj facade (close-out)
  - ARCHITECTURE.md: `src/jj.rs` joins the module
    inventory; the stale subcommand enumeration corrected
    (`finalize` dropped; `squash_push`, `revert`,
    `validate_bot`, `config_cmd`, `bot_session` /
    `transcript`, `sync/state` added)
  - per-stage refactor Todos: each remaining program stage
    is a short ranked entry pointing at its stage section
    (por → dual to todo-backlog); the umbrella entry
    shrinks to the program pointer
  - `## Done` entry; `## In Progress` retired; ladder
    commit refs pruned (the As-built ladder here is the
    permanent record)

### Outcome

Every read-only jj query spawn now goes through `src/jj.rs`
(log templates + bookmark listings), with one tracking
parser pair and one ochid trailer parser beside it, and one
jj test helper per crate. The remaining `run(` sites are
mutations (commit / describe / bookmark / push / fetch /
squash / rebase / op), the git/gh spawns owned by the
de-gitify-init stage and the by-design `gh` calls, push's
`jj diff --stat` display passthrough, and the deliberate
direct spawns in test harnesses — the surface the jj-lib
migration stage inherits.

## docs: adopt new template repo names

Commits: [[7]]

The template repos were renamed (`vc-template-x1` →
`vc-x1-work-repo-template`, `vc-template-x1.claude` →
`vc-x1-bot-repo-template`); the template side adopted the new
names 2026-07-21. Sweep vc-x1's live mentions to match and
restore shared-doc-set byte-identity.

- AGENTS.md re-syncs byte-identical with the template's copy
  (the `CargoRust.toml` mention was the one divergent hunk)
- README.md init examples: the real templates no longer
  satisfy the `<CODE>.claude` sibling default, so the sweep
  makes that example an explicit `CODE,BOT` pair; a generic
  `../tmpl` example keeps the default documented
- Historical records keep the old name (`## Done` entries,
  the notes-sync manifest, chores, jj-tips) — GitHub
  redirects renamed repos. `src/init/tests.rs` fixture names
  are arbitrary strings, not repo references, and stay.

## docs: notes rework + config refresh

Commits:

Alignment batch riding the template-side session: vc-x1
adopts what the working pair (vc-x1 + the template) decided.

- `.vc-config.toml` (both sides) now carries init's
  generated optional-keys block, so the hand-seeded configs
  are byte-identical with what `vc-x1 init` emits (the block
  is schema-sourced and cannot drift from the binary).
- jj-tips.md re-syncs with the template under a
  reclassification: its recorded transcripts are pedagogy,
  not history, so example names adopt
  `vc-x1-work-repo-template`. This supersedes the previous
  section's "historical records keep the old name" for this
  one file; chores files and the notes-sync manifest remain
  historical and keep old names.
- The template-restructure design (single template repo +
  fixed bot seed manifest, `<CODE>.claude` sibling default
  dropped) is promoted to Todo #10; the `.bot` default-name
  decision and the symmetric config schema fold into the
  refactor program's facade-owns-topology stage.
- New Idea recorded: chores retire into a session index
  once the provenance viewer can present session + code
  (the template already seeds no chores history — a new
  project's history is its commits + bot session).

Review of your changes — all good, one nit fixed, everything validated:

- **`.vc-config.toml`** (work + bot side): the appended commented block is byte-for-byte what `render_optional_keys_block()` in `src/init.rs` generates (schema-sourced, drift-proof), and the vc-x1 and template copies share identical blobs. The bot-repo-template's config was *not* updated — presumably fine since Todo #10 retires that repo anyway, but flagging it.
- **Template-side chores-01** turned out to be more than the earlier rename: it's now emptied into a convention stub — which is the right shape given your "template chores aren't history" rule, and the removed sections' content survives in the template's commits. `notes/README.md` "this template" → "this repo" reads correctly post-clone.
- **Your TODO/backlog/refactor-doc rework** is coherent: backlog entries 6/8/32 folded into the refactor program and the new Todo #10, refs pruned cleanly (no dangling `[[4]]`), both files renumber-validate. I fixed one slip — the backlog lost its blank line before `# References`.

I then assembled the commit: chores-14 gets the section for this commit plus the `Commits:` backfill for the previous push; TODO.md gets the Done entry (`[11]`); version 0.73.2; full cargo cycle green (fmt, clippy, 491 tests, install — `vc-x1 -V` reports 0.73.2).

**Title:** `docs: notes rework + config refresh`

**Body:**
```
Batch of notes and config updates from the template-alignment
session: the template-restructure design lands in TODO, the
generated .vc-config.toml block is adopted verbatim, and
jj-tips syncs with the template's rename.

- .vc-config.toml: append init's generated optional-keys
  block (schema-sourced comments; byte-identical with the
  bot repo's and the template's copies)
- TODO.md
  - Restructure templates promoted from the backlog to #10,
    redesigned: single template repo + fixed bot seed
    manifest; bot template retired; <CODE>.claude sibling
    default dropped; later ranks renumbered
  - new Ideas entry: chores retire into a session index
    (post-viewer)
  - Done entry for this commit
- notes/jj-tips.md: template-name rename synced from the
  template — reclassified as pedagogy, not historical
  record, superseding the previous commit's "historical
  records keep the old name" for this file
- notes/refactor-20260716.md: facade-owns-topology stage
  absorbs the symmetric config schema and the .bot
  default-name decision, plus the diagnostics-string audit
- notes/todo-backlog.md: entries 6/8/32 folded into the
  refactor program and Todo #10; renumbered; ref [4] pruned
- notes/chores/chores-14.md: section for this commit;
  Commits: backfilled for "docs: adopt new template repo
  names"
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.73.2
```

Confirm and I'll run `vc-x1 push refactor-vc-x1`. The template's own pending changes (config, chores-01 stub, README wording, jj-tips) push from that session — they're outside this repo's push.

file.content:
# vc-x1-bot-repo-template

This is the bot repo of a dual-repo convention for using
a bot to help in the development of a coding project. The goal
is that this bot repo contains the "why" and "how", while the
partner main repo contains the "what". The key to the convention
is each change is cross-referenced to the other. Thus there
is a coherent story of the development of the project across time.

This repo is the source template for the bot side:
[vc-x1](https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1) `init` copies its
contents — the licenses, this README.md, and the empty
`memory/MEMORY.md` — into a new project's `.claude` repo, then
generates the remaining minimum files (such as `.vc-config.toml`)
itself. `memory/MEMORY.md` is intentionally empty and expected to
stay empty; it is seeded here because Claude tends to create it
otherwise.

See [vc-x1-work-repo-template](https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1-work-repo-template)
for more details.

## License

Licensed under either of

- Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE) or http://apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)
- MIT license ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT) or http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)

### Contribution

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted
for inclusion in the work by you, as defined in the Apache-2.0 license, shall
be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Chores-14

Continuation of `chores-13.md` (closed at `0.72.x`, parked on
the `support-trapezoid-commits` bookmark). This file covers
the jj refactor program
([refactor-20260716.md](../refactor-20260716.md)) from
`0.73.0` onward, worked on the `refactor-vc-x1` bookmark
while `main` parks at the `0.71.0` tip.

Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`AGENTS.md`](../../AGENTS.md#reference-numbering); chores-14
starts at `[1]`.

## refactor: DRY jj facade

Commits: see [As-built ladder](#as-built-ladder)

~30 call sites hand-roll `run("jj", ["log", "-r", <rev>,
"--no-graph", "-T", <template>, "-R", <repo>])` and each
module has quietly grown a private wrapper — spotted at
0.69.0-2, where `jj_rev_exists` read as "first of its kind"
but was the Nth reinvention. One typed facade module ends
the reinvention; first stage of the refactor program.

### As-built ladder

- [[1]] 0.73.0-0 chore: open jj facade cycle
  - restore the five notes files from
    `support-trapezoid-commits` (TODO.md, todo-backlog,
    forks-multi-user, refactor doc, chores-13) so the shared
    bookkeeping rides the refactor line
  - re-plan after the pivot: `## In Progress` → the DRY
    facade ladder; the trapezoid `--merge [<base>]` design
    folded into the refactor doc's trapezoid stage; new
    "stateless push" stage (retire the push state file —
    from the extraction review's "why does `Stage` live in
    a file?")
  - shared-doc-sync todos updated: vc-template-x1 synced
    2026-07-20; family grows to five (adds zc-msg-x1,
    tprobe)
  - version 0.73.0-0; backfill the 0.72.0-1 ref [57] in
    chores-13; open this file
- [[2]] 0.73.0-1 refactor: jj facade query module
  - new `src/jj.rs`: the `log(repo, rev, template)` primitive
    plus typed helpers — `matches` / `rev_exists`, `chid_of`,
    `cid_of` / `cid_short_of`, `desc_of`, `is_empty` — every
    read-only `jj log -T` spawn now goes through it
  - folded: `squash_push::{jj_rev_exists, jj_commit_id,
    rev_is_empty_undescribed}` (the last two deleted, the
    empty-undescribed check rebuilt on `is_empty` +
    `desc_of`), `push::{get_change_id, jj_log_empty}`, the
    sanity verifiers' inline template blocks, `init::jj_chid`,
    `sync::{commit_id, revset_nonempty}` + the
    `commit_ids_of_bookmark` template, and
    `common::bookmark_publish_state`'s commit-id closure
  - `rev_exists` folds jj's unresolvable-revision errors to
    `false` (the `try_commit_id` patterns); the push
    stale-state checks keep their any-failure-is-stale
    behavior via `.unwrap_or(false)` at the call sites
- [[3]] 0.73.0-2 refactor: jj facade tracking parse
  - the two tracking parsers unify on one listing:
    `jj::bookmark_list` / `bookmark_list_all` join the
    facade, and `common::find_tracked_remote` (positive:
    indented `@remote` entry, synced or divergent form)
    sits beside `find_non_tracking_remote` (negative:
    column-0 ref) over the same `-a` output
  - `main::bm_track_one` drops its raw
    `std::process::Command` spawn + private parse — now a
    thin wrapper; `verify_tracking` and squash-push's
    bookmark-existence check use the facade
  - five `find_tracked_remote` unit tests
  - rider: TODO.md In Progress ladder rungs now carry
    prepended `[[N]]` commit refs (backfilled once pushed,
    like the chores As-built rungs); intro template updated
- [[4]] 0.73.0-3 refactor: jj facade ochid parse
  - `desc_helpers::extract_ochids` (column-0 trailers, all,
    in order, values trimmed) becomes the crate's one
    string-level ochid parser; `extract_ochid_from_desc` is
    now `.pop()` over it (the *last* trailer — matching
    `common::extract_ochid`'s previous reverse scan) and
    `common::extract_ochid` wraps that over
    `commit.description()`
  - `squash_push`'s local `extract_ochids` deleted; its
    three parser tests move to desc_helpers, plus a new
    multi-ochid last-wins test
  - behavior tightened: the single-value accessors are now
    column-0-only — an indented `ochid:` mention is prose,
    not a trailer (previously the trimmed scans matched it)
- [[5]] 0.73.0-4 test: jj facade fixture helpers
  - `test_helpers::jj_ok` (which anticipated this migration
    in its doc note) gains `cid` / `chid` / `description`;
    the near-identical private copies in
    `push/integration_tests.rs` and
    `sync/integration_tests.rs` are deleted (push keeps a
    local `desc_first_line` built on `jj_ok`)
  - deviation from the plan bullet: `tests/cli_sync.rs`
    lives in its own test crate and can't reach the
    binary's `test_helpers`, and its variant needs `HOME`
    injection — its `jj` / `cid` move to the crate's shared
    `tests/common/mod.rs` instead, with the why documented
  - the shared helpers spawn directly rather than calling
    `crate::jj`, so test inspection stays independent of
    the facade under test
- [[6]] 0.73.0 refactor: DRY jj facade (close-out)
  - ARCHITECTURE.md: `src/jj.rs` joins the module
    inventory; the stale subcommand enumeration corrected
    (`finalize` dropped; `squash_push`, `revert`,
    `validate_bot`, `config_cmd`, `bot_session` /
    `transcript`, `sync/state` added)
  - per-stage refactor Todos: each remaining program stage
    is a short ranked entry pointing at its stage section
    (por → dual to todo-backlog); the umbrella entry
    shrinks to the program pointer
  - `## Done` entry; `## In Progress` retired; ladder
    commit refs pruned (the As-built ladder here is the
    permanent record)

### Outcome

Every read-only jj query spawn now goes through `src/jj.rs`
(log templates + bookmark listings), with one tracking
parser pair and one ochid trailer parser beside it, and one
jj test helper per crate. The remaining `run(` sites are
mutations (commit / describe / bookmark / push / fetch /
squash / rebase / op), the git/gh spawns owned by the
de-gitify-init stage and the by-design `gh` calls, push's
`jj diff --stat` display passthrough, and the deliberate
direct spawns in test harnesses — the surface the jj-lib
migration stage inherits.

## docs: adopt new template repo names

Commits: [[7]]

The template repos were renamed (`vc-template-x1` →
`vc-x1-work-repo-template`, `vc-template-x1.claude` →
`vc-x1-bot-repo-template`); the template side adopted the new
names 2026-07-21. Sweep vc-x1's live mentions to match and
restore shared-doc-set byte-identity.

- AGENTS.md re-syncs byte-identical with the template's copy
  (the `CargoRust.toml` mention was the one divergent hunk)
- README.md init examples: the real templates no longer
  satisfy the `<CODE>.claude` sibling default, so the sweep
  makes that example an explicit `CODE,BOT` pair; a generic
  `../tmpl` example keeps the default documented
- Historical records keep the old name (`## Done` entries,
  the notes-sync manifest, chores, jj-tips) — GitHub
  redirects renamed repos. `src/init/tests.rs` fixture names
  are arbitrary strings, not repo references, and stay.

## docs: notes rework + config refresh

Commits:

Alignment batch riding the template-side session: vc-x1
adopts what the working pair (vc-x1 + the template) decided.

- `.vc-config.toml` (both sides) now carries init's
  generated optional-keys block, so the hand-seeded configs
  are byte-identical with what `vc-x1 init` emits (the block
  is schema-sourced and cannot drift from the binary).
- jj-tips.md re-syncs with the template under a
  reclassification: its recorded transcripts are pedagogy,
  not history, so example names adopt
  `vc-x1-work-repo-template`. This supersedes the previous
  section's "historical records keep the old name" for this
  one file; chores files and the notes-sync manifest remain
  historical and keep old names.
- The template-restructure design (single template repo +
  fixed bot seed manifest, `<CODE>.claude` sibling default
  dropped) is promoted to Todo #10; the `.bot` default-name
  decision and the symmetric config schema fold into the
  refactor program's facade-owns-topology stage.
- New Idea recorded: chores retire into a session index
  once the provenance viewer can present session + code
  (the template already seeds no chores history — a new
  project's history is its commits + bot session).

Done. The bot repo now carries `LICENSE-APACHE` / `LICENSE-MIT` (verbatim from vc-x1-bot-repo-template — both byte-identical to the work template's `.claude` copies) and a `README.md` adapted from the template's: the generic dual-repo-convention paragraph and License section are verbatim, but the "This repo is the source template for the bot side…" paragraph — which would be false in vc-x1's live bot repo — became a short partner-repo pointer to vc-x1 and the work template. Flag me if you'd rather have the verbatim copy instead.

The chores section and Done entry now mention the seeding, and the commit body gains one bullet (full body otherwise as shown before):

```
- .claude (bot repo): LICENSE-APACHE / LICENSE-MIT seeded
  verbatim from vc-x1-bot-repo-template; README.md adapted
  from it (the template-self-referential paragraph replaced
  with a partner-repo pointer)
```

Everything is staged: work repo (8 files — config, TODO, jj-tips, refactor doc, backlog, chores-14, Cargo.toml/lock) and bot repo (config + the three seeded files) all ride one `vc-x1 push refactor-vc-x1`, title `docs: notes rework + config refresh`. Validation is green from before; the since-then changes are markdown/bot-repo only. Confirm and I'll push.

**Title:**

```
docs: notes rework + config refresh
```

**Body:**

```
Batch of notes and config updates from the template-alignment
session: the template-restructure design lands in TODO, the
generated .vc-config.toml block is adopted verbatim, jj-tips
syncs with the template's rename, and the bot repo gains its
seed-manifest files.

- .vc-config.toml: append init's generated optional-keys
  block (schema-sourced comments; byte-identical with the
  bot repo's and the template's copies)
- TODO.md
  - Restructure templates promoted from the backlog to #10,
    redesigned: single template repo + fixed bot seed
    manifest; bot template retired; <CODE>.claude sibling
    default dropped; later ranks renumbered
  - new Ideas entry: chores retire into a session index
    (post-viewer)
  - Done entry for this commit
- notes/jj-tips.md: template-name rename synced from the
  template — reclassified as pedagogy, not historical
  record, superseding the previous commit's "historical
  records keep the old name" for this file
- notes/refactor-20260716.md: facade-owns-topology stage
  absorbs the symmetric config schema and the .bot
  default-name decision, plus the diagnostics-string audit
- notes/todo-backlog.md: entries 6/8/32 folded into the
  refactor program and Todo #10; renumbered; ref [4] pruned
- notes/chores/chores-14.md: section for this commit;
  Commits: backfilled for "docs: adopt new template repo
  names"
- .claude (bot repo): LICENSE-APACHE / LICENSE-MIT seeded
  verbatim from vc-x1-bot-repo-template; README.md adapted
  from it (the template-self-referential paragraph replaced
  with a partner-repo pointer)
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.73.2
```

The `ochid:` trailer is injected by `vc-x1 push` itself. On your approval I'll run `vc-x1 push refactor-vc-x1`.
